BlockDAG IPFS Content Sidecar

The IPFS content sidecar makes finalized BlockDAG raw-datadir generations easier to find and fetch. The raw datadir sidecar seals immutable file/chunk generations with signed manifest roots; IPFS still must never decide chain trust from a CID alone.

Design Rules

  • ops/seal_rawdatadir_sidecar_content.py turns each completed sidecar rsync into immutable SHA-256 chunks plus a signed canonical manifest root.
  • The sealer reads BDAG_RAWDATADIR_SIGNING_KEY_FILE, defaulting to the same private key file provisioned for the IPFS segment writer. The key file is local private state and is not written into source or examples.
  • ops/ipfs_content_sidecar.py pins only an already-finalized signed artifact directory without a DO_NOT_PUBLISH marker.
  • ops/restore-rawdatadir-segment-artifact.py can reconstruct a raw node datadir from a local, SSH, or IPFS artifact source. IPFS restore requires a raw content index or artifact CID and a trusted signer roster.
  • Raw datadir content generation remains owned by ops/maintain-rawdatadir-sidecar.sh and ops/seal_rawdatadir_sidecar_content.py.
  • Final publishability remains gated by artifact finalization, sidecar content sealing, manifest signatures, and consensus validation.
  • Background work runs only after background_maintenance_decision() allows it; pressure defers the tick rather than disabling future attempts.
  • Raw-datadir sidecar safety is recorded with each attempt, but it is not a permanent gate that blocks pinning an already-finalized safe artifact.
  • DO_NOT_PUBLISH markers and unsigned manifests fail closed by default.
  • The generated latest index records the artifact CID and manifest hash, but receivers must still verify the signed manifest root against BDAG_RAWDATADIR_TRUSTED_SIGNERS, chunk/file hashes, network, genesis, tip, state root, and then catch up through normal consensus.

Modes

  • BDAG_IPFS_CONTENT_SIDECAR_MODE=off: disabled.
  • BDAG_IPFS_CONTENT_SIDECAR_MODE=auto: timer wakes lazily, retries after pressure clears, and pins only when all gates pass and a finalized safe artifact exists.
  • BDAG_RAWDATADIR_SIDECAR_CONTENT_MODE=auto: rawdatadir sidecar syncs also seal immutable file/chunk generations.
  • BDAG_IPFS_RAWDATADIR_RESTORE_PRESTART=1: the release installer restores an empty mainnet datadir from a trusted IPFS raw artifact when BDAG_IPFS_RAWDATADIR_RESTORE_ARTIFACT_CID, BDAG_IPFS_RAWDATADIR_RESTORE_INDEX_CID, or BDAG_IPFS_RAWDATADIR_RESTORE_INDEX_FILE is configured.

Release Safety

Installer logic keeps retry timers active in auto mode. The services self-defer when mining pressure, unsafe storage, missing finalization, unsigned manifests, or DO_NOT_PUBLISH markers make work unsafe. Single active-node mining systems remain fail-closed for publication unless a finalized artifact already exists and resource pressure has cleared.

Status

The raw checkpoint sidecar writes ops/runtime/ipfs-content-sidecar-status.json and ops/runtime/ipfs-content/rawdatadir-content-index.json. The chain-order segment writer owns ops/runtime/ipfs-content/latest-index.json. Dashboard/watchdog code can consume those files without probing IPFS on every refresh.

Pre-start raw IPFS restore writes ops/runtime/ipfs-content/rawdatadir-restore-status.json. The chain-order segment restore drill writes ops/runtime/ipfs-content/restore-drill-status.json and remains non-destructive.

Permanent Discovery

Future systems should read ops/ipfs-content-discovery.json first. It records the durable latest pointer /ipns/k51qzi5uqu5djjlh4vxtmzyswx0qk4s3wdlf3yrpkszp38gq5sl71zcgmmc3jk and the current immutable segment-index CID. A raw-datadir content index is a separate bdag_ipfs_content_index_v1 document; do not feed a chain-order segment index into the raw restore path. The protocol design is recorded in docs/ipfs-append-only-segment-protocol.html.
